rationale: The citing paper’s core process claim is that discount framing changes hedonic-bundle choice via reduced anticipated guilt/purchase justification, while the cited paper studies licensing mechanisms for indulgent (luxury vs. necessity) choices and related self-concept/guilt dynamics. That construct-level overlap makes the cited work a plausible theory/mechanism source rather than an obvious incidental mention. Even though the raw evidence is only a single DOI match without quote context, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
